Christoforos Hadjigeorgiou commented:
Hi Louis,
For MPI and compilers those are already loaded as part of the default environment.
For HDF5 and FFTW you will also need to load and set some environment variables:
module load fftw-3.3.6-pl2-intel-17.0.4-45kexpw
module load hdf5-1.10.1-intel-17.0.4-acprvzg
export FFTW_PATH=/usr/local/software/spack/spack-0.11.2/opt/spack/linux-rhel7-x86_64/intel-17.0.4/fftw-3.3.6-pl2-45kexpwgmgvqnuq4yse2vjee3hbvxnyb
export FFTW_LIBRARY_PATH=$FFTW_PATH/lib
export FFTW_INCLUDE_PATH=$FFTW_PATH/include
export MPI_PATH=/usr/local/Cluster-Apps/intel/2017.4/compilers_and_libraries_2017.4.196/linux/mpi/intel64
export MPI_LIBRARY_PATH=$MPI_PATH/lib
export MPI_INCLUDE_PATH=$MPI_PATH/include
Some environment variables may be unnecessary but I set them to avoid any complains during install.
To install dedalus using pip in the virtual environment:
pip install dedalus
To install dedalus manually I ran while inside the venv:
hg clone [https://bitbucket.org/dedalus-project/dedaluscd](https://bitbucket.org/dedalus-project/dedaluscd) dedalus
pip install -r ./requirements
pip install .
1. These two steps may be unnecessary, you can try running dedalus without them first
 python3 ./setup.py build_ext --inplace 
 python3 ./setup.py install
 
To run dedalus for both pip and manual installation I did:
LD_PRELOAD=$MPI_PATH/lib/libmpi.so python3
Without the preload FFTW complains about not finding MPI functions.
Let us know how it goes.
Kind regards,
Chris

"Permalink to this headline")

Since the cpu-r-hbm nodes are running Rocky 9, you will want to recompile
your applications if they are coming from a different operating system or a different OS version.

It is possible to log into a login node with compatible OS/software by logging into *login-sapphire.hpc.cam.ac.uk*,
which will land you on one of the *login-s-\** nodes.

Alternatively, request an interactive node using *sintr*:

```
sintr -t 1:0:0 -N1 -n38 -A YOURPROJECT-CPU -p ukaea-spr-hbm(-flat) -q INTR
```

to rebuild and re-optimise your applications to get the best from these specialist nodes. The `-q INTR` option used above requests a higher priority interactive jobs, but does not allow for more than 1h wall time. If you need a longer interactive session, you will need to remove the option `-q INTR` from your `sintr` command line and queue for longer.

The per-job wallclock time limits are 36 hours and 12 hours for SL1/2 and SL3
respectively.

The per-job, per-user cpu limits are now 4256 and 448 cpus for SL1/2
and SL3 respectively.

These limits should be regarded as provisional and may be revised.

### Default submission script for Sapphire Rapids[¶](#default-submission-script-for-sapphire-rapids "Permalink to this headline")

There is no default job submission template dedicated to Sapphire Rapid nodes, but you might be able to make one for yourself by tweaking an Icelake template.

user_input:
Good afternoon,
A student from CEB would like to use abaqus 2024 on the HPC cluster. The student mentioned that they contacted you and you advised that this version is not compatible.
I wanted to confirm if this is the case or did the end user misunderstand.
Kind regards,
Zac
------------------------------------------------------
Zac Iskander (he/him)
IT Support Team Leader
University of Cambridge
Department of Engineering
Trumpington Street
Cambridge, CB2 1PZ, UK
01223760591
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Software Version Support / Licensing
Technical Action Required: No (policy clarification)
Escalation Required: Yes (new build request if required)
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The immediate answer is that `abaqus/2024` is not currently available as a centrally supported version on the cluster; the latest supported central version is `abaqus/2022`. If there is a requirement for a newer version, licensing and a separate build or install request would need to be coordinated.

ACTION
1) Confirm that `abaqus/2022` is the latest supported central version.
2) Explain that no timeline can be promised for `abaqus/2024`.
3) If the relevant licence owner wants a newer centrally available version, ask them to submit a fresh build or install request to HPC support.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Zac,

At present the newest centrally supported version on the cluster is `abaqus/2022`, so the user did not misunderstand.

If the relevant licence owner wants a newer centrally available version, please send a new request to `support@hpc.cam.ac.uk` and we can look into building and providing that version.

Best wishes,
HPC Support
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Software / Version Compatibility [2]
Technical Action Required: Yes [2]
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is inquiring about the compatibility of Abaqus 2024 on the HPC cluster, and whether a previous response indicating that this version is not compatible was accurate [2].

EVIDENCE BASIS
This answer is driven by a mixed-evidence case, combining official documentation and a historic ticket. The official documentation does not explicitly state the supported versions of Abaqus [1], while the historic ticket suggests that Abaqus 2024 may not be compatible due to version incompatibility issues [2].

VERIFICATION NEEDED
- Confirm the currently supported versions of Abaqus on the HPC cluster.
- Verify whether Abaqus 2024 is compatible with the current HPC environment.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you provide more information about the previous response regarding Abaqus 2024 compatibility?
- Are there any specific requirements or workflows that necessitate the use of Abaqus 2024?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
The retrieved evidence suggests that there may be compatibility issues with Abaqus 2024 on the HPC cluster [2]. However, we need to verify the currently supported versions of Abaqus to provide a definitive answer.
Please let us know if you have any further information about the previous response, and we will investigate this matter further [2].
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not infer current supported versions from older tickets when current official documentation is available [1][2].

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/getting_support.html::chunk::0000
[2] : HPCSSUP-91919

The University of Cambridge provides support for users on CSD3 and can be contacted via email at support@hpc.cam.ac.uk. This will open a ticket with the support team.

Tip

Each support request should be submitted as a *brand new* email to the address above. If you reopen a previously closed ticket, your request may be missed because it won’t reach the active service desk agent.

To help us help you, please provide as much information as possible in your ticket. At a minimum, please include:

* Your name
* Your account/PI
* your job submission script
* the working directories or paths you are using (e.g. `/rds/user/$USER/hpc-work/foo/bar/`), and
* as much detail as possible on what you are working on, what you have tried, and error messages you receive.

## Use of AI Tools and Online Resources[¶](#use-of-ai-tools-and-online-resources "Permalink to this headline")

We understand that as AI assistants such as ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini increase in popularity, our users may consult them for assistance with technical issues on our platforms. We have compiled in this section some suggestions on how to safely work with these tools as well as how to engage the service desk.

Before acting on any AI-generated advice, please take a moment to review our guidance below on how to use these tools safely and responsibly.

Note

Much of the advice that follows is equally applicable to non-AI generated content found on the internet.

Since the cpu-r-hbm nodes are running Rocky 9, you will want to recompile
your applications if they are coming from a different operating system or a different OS version.

It is possible to log into a login node with compatible OS/software by logging into *login-sapphire.hpc.cam.ac.uk*,
which will land you on one of the *login-s-\** nodes.

Alternatively, request an interactive node using *sintr*:

```
sintr -t 1:0:0 -N1 -n38 -A YOURPROJECT-CPU -p ukaea-spr-hbm(-flat) -q INTR
```

to rebuild and re-optimise your applications to get the best from these specialist nodes. respectively.

and SL3 respectively.

These limits should be regarded as provisional and may be revised.

The [Cambridge Service for Data Driven Discovery (CSD3)](https://www.csd3.cam.ac.uk) is one of the [EPSRC](http://www.epsrc.ac.uk)
Tier2 National HPC Services hosted by [Research Computing Services](https://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk) at the [University of Cambridge](https://www.cam.ac.uk).

The facility became live in November 2017 and began the next chapter in HPC for the University of Cambridge. It was upgraded
in 2020, and again in 2021 and 2023.

CSD3 is currently a combination of two platform types:

* Intel Xeon Scalable Processors (Cascade Lake, Ice Lake and Sapphire Rapids CPUs)
* NVIDIA A100 GPUs.

All CSD3 nodes are currently interconnected by Mellanox HDR
InfiniBand. The GPU sub-cluster is also known as Wilkes3-GPU in its
own right. The name “Peta4” was associated historically with the
original CPU elements of CSD3.

user_input:
Hi Team,
I’m getting in touch regarding an issue on the SRCP platform, which I’ve attached for reference. We urgently need the SNPolisher R package installed into the environment associated with the following platform ID: kjtcs2jvzw7.
This is time‑critical, as the NHSBT Genomics Programme is currently unable to carry out essential clinical work without this package being available.
Could you please prioritise this installation at your earliest opportunity and confirm once it has been completed?
Grateful
[cid:b9547aa4-5bed-4828-8cca-804065ce11a4]
Many thanks,
Justin Ofosu-Dankwa, PhD
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: SRCP / Package Deployment / R Module
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: High

QUICK ASSESSMENT
This is an urgent request to make the SNPolisher package available on a specific SRCP platform. The correct initial support path is to prioritise escalation to the SRCP/platform team for package installation. Once availability is confirmed, the next step is to validate the package in a plain R session before troubleshooting any surrounding workflow/tool integration.

ACTION
1) Escalate the request to the SRCP/platform team as a priority package-installation request.
2) Once the package is reported available, validate it in a plain R session.
3) If the user's external tool still fails after installation, identify the tool name and R path/environment it uses.
4) Update the user once installation status has been confirmed.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Dear Justin,

We are treating this as a priority request and are arranging installation of SNPolisher with the SRCP/platform team.

Once the package is available, we will send you the relevant load instructions and, if needed, follow up with checks to make sure your workflow is using the correct R environment.

Best regards,
HPC Support

Various versions of the R environment are available on CSD3. The latest
versions are `r/4.1.3/gcc/intel-oneapi-mkl/7dubq4gn` (GTK library support), `r/4.1.3/gcc/intel-oneapi-mkl/wwmppxe3` (vanilla) on the `icelake` partition and `r/4.4.0/gcc/3z5n2tph` on the `cclake` partition.

R can be run interactively by simply calling `R`, which will give a standard
R shell. This can be used for testing or setting up larger runs. However for
use inside the job scheduler it will generally be more effective to record the
R script in a file `analysis.R` and call it from inside a slurm batch script
`Rscript analysis.R`. For more examples of how to use slurm and write an
appropriate batch script please see [Running](../user-guide/quickstart.html#running).

## Installing R packages[¶](#installing-r-packages "Permalink to this headline")

While a few R packages have been centrally installed on CSD3 (do `module avail r-` to see a list), it is often necessary to install extra R packages into your own environment. For example to install cellranger, one can use:

```
$ R
R version...
[...]
> install.packages("cellranger")
```

which will download and build the package from CRAN.

If you run into build errors, the first fix is to tell R where to find the correct compiler. This can be done by creating the file `~/.R/Makevars` in your home directory and adding:

```
CC = gcc
CXX = g++
CXX11 = g++
FC = gfortran
F77 = gfortran
F90 = gfortran
```

which tells R to use the system compilers. "Permalink to this headline")

Since the cpu-r-hbm nodes are running Rocky 9, you will want to recompile
your applications if they are coming from a different operating system or a different OS version.

It is possible to log into a login node with compatible OS/software by logging into *login-sapphire.hpc.cam.ac.uk*,
which will land you on one of the *login-s-\** nodes.

Alternatively, request an interactive node using *sintr*:

```
sintr -t 1:0:0 -N1 -n38 -A YOURPROJECT-CPU -p ukaea-spr-hbm(-flat) -q INTR
```

to rebuild and re-optimise your applications to get the best from these specialist nodes. The `-q INTR` option used above requests a higher priority interactive jobs, but does not allow for more than 1h wall time. If you need a longer interactive session, you will need to remove the option `-q INTR` from your `sintr` command line and queue for longer.

The per-job wallclock time limits are 36 hours and 12 hours for SL1/2 and SL3
respectively.

The per-job, per-user cpu limits are now 4256 and 448 cpus for SL1/2
and SL3 respectively.

These limits should be regarded as provisional and may be revised.

### Default submission script for Sapphire Rapids[¶](#default-submission-script-for-sapphire-rapids "Permalink to this headline")

There is no default job submission template dedicated to Sapphire Rapid nodes, but you might be able to make one for yourself by tweaking an Icelake template.

user_input:
Hi
I've been getting an error on Ampere jobs recently where it fails immediately, stating that the CUDA-capable devices aren't available. I've had this error occur when using submission scripts for running turbostream 3.6.10 which has worked for years, and using a separate script running turbostream 4.2.127 recently. I've attached an example slurm.out file I've had from a turbostream4 job. This error seems to happen whenever I get assigned gpu-q-29, though I've heard colleagues mention getting something similar recently on different nodes. I'm aware I can exclude gpu-q-29 in my sbatch, but if it happens on other nodes and I lose all the time I waited in the queue, it's frustrating. Are there any sbatch instructions I can add to avoid this happening, or a fix for the route problem?
Many Thanks,
Dom

reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: GPU Runtime Failure / Node Health / Slurm Configuration
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The job is intermittently failing on a specific GPU node with CUDA device availability errors. Two grounded mitigations from the submitted command/output are to ensure the correct Ampere base module stack is loaded and to let Open MPI infer task placement from Slurm instead of forcing `-npernode` and `-np`.

ACTION
1) Load the correct base stack:
   `module purge`
   `module load rhel8/default-amp`
2) Remove manual `mpirun -npernode ... -np ...` overrides where possible.
3) Prefer `--tasks-per-node=4` rather than only `--ntasks=8` for the reported two-node case.
4) As a temporary workaround, route around the bad node with:
   `#SBATCH --exclude=gpu-q-29`
5) If the issue persists after these changes or starts affecting other nodes, escalate for node-health investigation.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hi Dom,

Please first make sure your script loads `rhel8/default-amp`, as that adds the expected CUDA runtime environment.

We also recommend removing the explicit `-npernode`/`-np` arguments and letting Open MPI infer task placement from Slurm. For a two-node, eight-task job, `--tasks-per-node=4` is the safer Slurm-side request.

In the short term, you can avoid the problematic node with:
`#SBATCH --exclude=gpu-q-29`

Best wishes,
HPC Support

The error message "all CUDA-capable devices are busy or unavailable" suggests a node-specific issue, particularly since the problem seems to occur consistently on `gpu-q-29` [2]. This could indicate a problem with the CUDA resources on that node.

This is a mixed-evidence case. The official documentation provides general guidance on submitting jobs to the Ampere nodes [1], while the historic tickets suggest that node-specific issues can cause this error [2][7]. The retrieved evidence does not confirm whether the issue is specific to `gpu-q-29` or if it could be a more general problem with the Ampere nodes.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)

- Are there any other error messages or logs that could provide more information about the issue?

## Recommendations for running on ampere[¶](#recommendations-for-running-on-ampere "Permalink to this headline")

Since the gpu-q nodes are running Rocky8, you will want to recompile
your applications. We suggest you do this by requesting an
[interactive node](#software).

The per-job wallclock time limits are 36 hours and 12 hours for SL1/2
and SL3 respectively.

The per-job, per-user GPU limits are currently 64 and 32 GPUs for SL1/2
and SL3 respectively.

These limits should be regarded as provisional and may be revised.

### Default submission script for ampere[¶](#default-submission-script-for-ampere "Permalink to this headline")

You should find a symbolic link to an Ampere default job submission script modified for the ampere nodes in your home directory, called:

```
slurm_submit.wilkes3
```

This is set up for non-MPI jobs, but can be modified for other types
of job. If you prefer to modify your existing job scripts, please see
the following sections for guidance.

### Jobs requiring N GPUs where N < 4[¶](#jobs-requiring-n-gpus-where-n-4 "Permalink to this headline")

Although there are 4 GPUs in each node it is possible to request fewer than this, e.g. to request 3 GPUs use:

```
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --gres=gpu:3
#SBATCH -p ampere
```

Slurm will enforce allocation of a proportional number of CPUs (32) per GPU.

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]

#SBATCH --gres=gpu:N

where 1 <= N <= 4.

Each GPU node contains 4 NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs, with 1000GB host RAM and two AMD EPYC 64-core processors.

Any jobs requesting more than one node must request 4 GPUs per node. Jobs less than one node in size will be prevented from requesting more than 3 CPUs per GPU. The enforcement is performed by a job submission filter which will produce an explanatory message if it rejects a job outright.

### Submitting the job to the queuing system[¶](#submitting-the-job-to-the-queuing-system "Permalink to this headline")

The command sbatch is used to submit jobs, e.g.

```
sbatch submission_script
```

The command will return a unique job identifier, which is used to query and control the job and to identify output. See the man page (*man sbatch*) for more options.

The following more complex example submits a job array with index values between 1 and 7 with a step size of 2 (i.e. 1, 3, 5 and 7) to the project STARS-SL2-CPU:

```
sbatch --array=1-7:2 -A STARS-SL2-CPU submission_script
```

### Deleting jobs[¶](#deleting-jobs "Permalink to this headline")

To cancel a job (either running or still queuing) use scancel:

```
scancel <jobid>
```

The <jobid> is printed when the job is submitted, alternatively use the commands squeue, qstat or showq to obtain the job ID.

user_input:
Dear HPC team,
Please find attached a purchase order for 2,000 CPU hours of service level 2 , to be credited to Emily Lines.
Best wishes,
Anne Baranger
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: SL2 Credit Provisioning / Procurement Workflow
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The requester has sent a PO for SL2 CPU hours to be credited for Emily Lines. From the initial message alone, it is not clear whether these hours should go onto an existing SL2 project or whether a new project account is needed. The correct next step is to confirm the target SL2 account and, if none exists, follow the normal project-creation and approval path.

ACTION
1) Ask whether the credits are for an existing SL2 project or a new one.
2) If no suitable existing SL2 CPU project exists, follow the normal approval process to create one.
3) Add the required users to the target project once the account path is confirmed.
4) If an existing account is intended instead, request the exact target project code.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Anne,

Before we can apply the purchased CPU hours, we need to confirm whether they are for an existing SL2 CPU project or whether a new project needs to be created.

If there is no suitable existing SL2 CPU project for this work, we would need to follow the normal approval process before creating one and applying the hours.

Best regards,
HPC Support

Category: Purchase Order / CPU Hours [1][2][4][6][8]
Technical Action Required: Yes [2][4][6][8]
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user has submitted a purchase order for 2,000 CPU hours of service level 2 to be credited to Emily Lines. The retrieved evidence suggests that the HPC team should process the purchase order and add the CPU hours to the relevant account [2][4][6][8].

EVIDENCE BASIS
This is a mixed-evidence case. The official documentation provides context on the service levels and usage policies [1][3][5][7], while the historic tickets inform the workflow for processing purchase orders and adding CPU hours to accounts [2][4][6][8]. The retrieved evidence supports the claim that the HPC team should process the purchase order and add the CPU hours to the relevant account.

VERIFICATION NEEDED
- Confirm the details of the purchase order, including the account name and the number of CPU hours to be added [2][4][6][8].
- Verify that the purchase order has been approved and that the necessary funds are available [2][4][6][8].
- Check the current balance of the account and ensure that the additional CPU hours can be added [2][4][6][8].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you please provide more information about the purchase order, such as the date and the approval status? [2][4][6][8]
- Are there any specific requirements or restrictions for the use of the additional CPU hours? [2][4][6][8]

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,

Thank you for submitting the purchase order for 2,000 CPU hours of service level 2. We will process the order and add the CPU hours to the relevant account [2][4][6][8].

Please note that we may need to verify some details of the purchase order, such as the account name and the approval status [2][4][6][8]. If you have any questions or concerns, please do not hesitate to contact us.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- The HPC team should ensure that the purchase order is properly approved and that the necessary funds are available before adding the CPU hours to the account [2][4][6][8].
- The additional CPU hours should be added to the relevant account in accordance with the service level agreement and the usage policies [1][3][5][7].

The service allows a range of service levels with different features. These are associated with different quality of service definitions within the scheduler, enforcing different job priorities, maximum job sizes and maximum run times.

A service level is attached to a project, which is a group of users led by a principal investigator (PI) who controls (or who can apply for) a line of funding for HPC.

## Service Levels[¶](#service-levels "Permalink to this headline")

The current Service Levels (SLs), which came into operation in November 2017 and which apply to the Peta4 and Wilkes3 clusters, are described below.

Funding units are in the form of usage credits. What these credits represent depends on the type of cluster. For the CPU cluster (Peta4) 1 credit = 1 CPU core for 1 hour. For the GPU cluster (Wilkes3) 1 credit = 1 GPU for 1 hour.

Accounting periods or quarters are three month periods of the year running 1st February - 30th April, 1st May - 31st July, 1st August - 31st October and 1st November - 31st January.

## Service Level 1 – Guaranteed Usage[¶](#service-level-1-guaranteed-usage "Permalink to this headline")

Service Level 1 (SL1) operates with the highest quality of service QOS1, designed for groups which require large and steady amounts of computer time over a long period.

## Service Level 3 – Free Usage[¶](#service-level-3-free-usage "Permalink to this headline")

Service Level 3 (SL3) operates with the medium quality of service, QOS2. QOS2 is lower than QOS1 which is used in SL1 & SL2. This service level is designed for groups with medium usage requirements who currently do not have funding to pay for their usage, thus an immediate conversion of funds into credits is not required.

SL3 is capped with a maximum number of usage credits per PI per quarter. This has been introduced to promote a more even usage of the free time on the system. Currently each PI may receive **200,000 CPU core hours** and **3000 GPU hours** per quarter.

There is no guaranteed minimum usage level for SL3 and there is no concept of expiry or of moving core hours across quarters.

Once a group in SL3 has consumed all its allowed core hours in a quarter, jobs can still be submitted at Service Level 4.

This lists the charge for each job in the CompHrs column. Since this example queries usage of a -CPU project, these are CPU core hours. Similarly, for a -GPU project they would be GPU hours.

**I would like to add core hours to a particular member of my group**

```
gdeposit -z 10000 -p halos-sl2-spqr1-gpu
```

This coordinator of the HALOS-SL2-GPU might use this to add 10000 GPU hours to the HALOS-SL2-SPQR1-GPU subproject assigned to the user spqr1. Note that if a compute hour limit applies to the parent of the project in the project hierarchy - i.e. if the parent project HALOS-SL2-GPU has an overall compute hour limit (which it almost certainly does) - then the global limit will still apply across all per-user projects.

Compute hours may be added to a project by a designated project coordinator user. Reducing the compute hours available to a project is also possible by adding a negative number of hours via the –time= syntax, e.g. the following command undoes the above:

```
gdeposit --time=-10000 -p halos-sl2-spqr1-gpu
```

user_input:
Hi there,
I manage a Research Data store for the “kstarkstar” project:
Directory: rds-mk652-kstarkstar
Project ID: bUoO2gzww9Q
It is about to expire. I want to extend the license for another year (for all 5TB) with all of the same permissions. I raised a PO have requested a new data store but think I may have made a mistake here. I don’t want a new storage area, I just want to extend the existing one.
Please can you resolve my mistake?
The PO for the extension is attached.
Many thanks,
Matt
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage Renewal / Procurement Correction
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user wants to extend an existing storage project but believes they may have accidentally requested a new storage purchase instead. The safe handling is to stop them from activating anything new until support has verified which service record should be extended and whether the mistaken request can be corrected or needs to be cancelled or reissued.

ACTION
1) Ask the user not to activate the mistaken newly purchased storage project.
2) Confirm which existing project is the one that should be extended.
3) Check internally whether the mistaken request can be removed or converted.
4) If cancellation or reissue is required, direct the user to the appropriate purchasing or finance route.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hi Matt,

Please leave the accidentally purchased storage project unallocated while we verify whether the mistaken request can be corrected or whether it needs to be cancelled and reissued.

Once we confirm the correct path, we will tell you whether any purchasing or finance action is needed on your side.

Best wishes,
HPC Support

Category: Storage / Renewal Workflow [1][2][3][4][5][6][7][8]
Technical Action Required: Yes [2][5][6]
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user has raised a PO for storage renewal but believes it may have been attached to the wrong project, resulting in a new storage area being created instead of extending the existing one [1][2][5][6].

EVIDENCE BASIS
This is a mixed-evidence case. The official documentation provides general guidance on storage renewal and management [2][4][8], while historic tickets offer case-specific workflow context [1][3][5][6][7]. The retrieved evidence suggests that the user has attempted to renew their storage but may have incorrectly created a new project instead of extending the existing one.

VERIFICATION NEEDED
- Confirm the project ID and directory name of the existing storage project [1][5].
- Verify the status of the new storage project created in error [5][6].
- Check the PO details and ensure it is correctly associated with the intended storage project [1][2][5].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you please provide the project ID and directory name of the existing storage project you intended to renew [1][5]?
- Have you attached the PO to the correct project in the storage portal [2][5]?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,

The retrieved evidence suggests that you have attempted to renew your storage but may have incorrectly created a new project instead of extending the existing one [1][2][5][6]. 

To resolve this, please confirm the project ID and directory name of the existing storage project you intended to renew, and verify the status of the new storage project created in error [1][5][6]. We will then assist you in correcting the association of the PO with the intended storage project [1][2][5].

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- When renewing storage, ensure that the PO is correctly associated with the intended storage project to avoid creating a new project in error [1][2][5][6].
- Verify the project ID and directory name of the existing storage project to ensure accurate renewal [1][5].

Important

The School of Biological Sciences ‘SBS’ resource allocation scheme ended on 31 Dec 2022.

Any PIs and users still wishing to retain their current storage projects should respond to the automated renewal notices sent from the self-service portal prior to 31 Dec. Or alternatively, contact the servicedesk at support@hpc.cam.ac.uk for assistance in identifying the relevant projects or migrating the data elsewhere.

## Key Deadlines[¶](#key-deadlines "Permalink to this headline")

### From 1 January 2023[¶](#from-1-january-2023 "Permalink to this headline")

* Any storage projects not yet renewed have been flagged as ‘suspended’ in the
  storage portal.
* Data owners and managers can still generate quotes and submit purchase orders to renew,
  which will be automatically backdated to be effective from 1 Jan.
* Access to data is still possible during the 30 day grace period for late renewals,
  after which access to the data will be lost.

### From 30 January 2023 (end of grace period)[¶](#from-30-january-2023-end-of-grace-period "Permalink to this headline")

* User access to unrenewed projects will be lost.
* All data in projects will **only be accessible on request**.
  Access will be provided in a read-only mode in cases where it is necessary to migrate
  data to an alternative location on CSD3 or offsite.

Warning

The WBIC platform contained a number of different storage areas for
users, all of which will be **retired** with the closure of the platform.

All data should be migrated to an alternative storage platform on
CSD3 or downloaded to another location before the platform is
shut-down.

Below we outline alternative services on CSD3 for each storage area that
are available for purchase so that you can replicate the WBIC
environment as best as possible.

#### /lustre[¶](#lustre "Permalink to this headline")

The WBIC dedicated Lustre filesystem (/lustre) is replaced by our [RDS
storage service](https://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/research-data-store)
which provides equivalent large-scale scratch storage.

All CSD3 users get a free 1TB personal scratch space, but all
group-shared capacity must be purchased through our [Storage self-service
gateway](https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk/storage/RDS/)
Owners of group directories will be contacted separately to make arrangements.

#### /home[¶](#home "Permalink to this headline")

All WBIC users had 100GB of /home space, however on CSD3 this is reduced
to 40GB. Users with usage greater than 40GB will be required to either
reduce their usage under this amount, or to purchase additional RFS or
RDS storage and move excess data to these areas.

#### /data[¶](#data "Permalink to this headline")

WBIC users also had access to the /data storage location, which benefited
from the same resilient storage platform as /home, including hourly
snapshots and data replication, designed for datasets requiring durable
backups.

Eg: data under /lustre/group,
/lustre/archive or any group-owned data under other locations.

This data will require being moved to an RDS (scratch), RCS
(archive) or RFS (resilient, backup) project on CSD3.

Group data should be purchased as a group, which would typically be
done by the PI for your research group. Additionally, given the
quantities of data involved, we can perform the data migration for
your group so that it is carried out as efficiently as possible.

Important

Please encourage your PI to get in touch with us at
support@hpc.cam.ac.uk to discuss how we can assist the setup and
migration of group data to CSD3.

We will be attempting to contact all known PIs to assist in this
process, but being proactive here can help ensure that we can make
this transition as seamless as possible for your group.

#### 3. Purchase any required storage projects on CSD3[¶](#purchase-any-required-storage-projects-on-csd3 "Permalink to this headline")

Purchases can be made via our [storage portal](https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk).
Projects greater than 100TB in size are eligible for a volume
pricing discount.

#### 4. Migrate individual files to CSD3[¶](#migrate-individual-files-to-csd3 "Permalink to this headline")

For relatively modest quantities of data less than 1TB, you should be
able to migrate these yourself with `rsync`. An example command to
use would be,

```
user@wbic-gate-3:~$ rsync --verbose --info=progress2 --archive --no-o --no-g \
